claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by gathering some basic information:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me:
- How many children you're asking about?
- The age(s) of the child/children?

This will help me guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex is 17 and Blake is 18. We all live together here in Birmingham.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children - Alex (17) and Blake (18), and you all live together in Birmingham.

Let me ask you some questions to assess eligibility for each child. Child Benefit eligibility is determined individually for each child, so I'll need to understand their specific circumstances.

Let me start with some important questions:

**For Alex (age 17):**
- Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications)?

**For Blake (age 18):**
- Is Blake currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?
- If yes, when did Blake turn 18 and when does their course end?

These questions are important because the age limits and education requirements differ for children over 16.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
For Alex, yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

For Blake, no, she's not in education anymore - she finished her studies and isn't doing any training at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information.

So to summarize:
- **Alex (17)** is in college doing A-levels (full-time non-advanced education)
- **Blake (18)** has finished her studies and is not in education or training

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

1. **Are you the person responsible for both Alex and Blake?** (meaning you're the one who looks after them and they live with you)

2. **Do you or your partner earn over £50,000 a year?** (This doesn't affect whether you can *claim* Child Benefit, but it may affect whether there's a tax charge - I want to make sure you have complete information)

3. **Are Alex or Blake currently:**
   - In hospital or local authority care for more than 12 weeks?
   - Living with someone else who might be claiming Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, I'm responsible for both of them - they both live with me and I look after them.

Regarding income, I'd prefer to just focus on whether I'm eligible first if that's alright.

And no, neither of them are in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming for them. They're just here at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. That's absolutely fine - we can focus on eligibility first.

Based on what you've told me, let me make sure I have all the key information:

**For Alex (17):**
- In full-time non-advanced education (A-levels)
- Lives with you
- You're responsible for them
- Not in hospital/care
- No one else is claiming for them

**For Blake (18):**
- Not in education or approved training
- Lives with you
- You're responsible for them
- Not in hospital/care
- No one else is claiming for them

Just one final important question: **Are you (or your partner if you have one) living or working in the UK, or receiving certain UK benefits?** This is about meeting the residence requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're all here in Birmingham as I mentioned.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me carefully review the eligibility criteria for each of your children:

**For Alex (age 17):**
- You are responsible for Alex and Alex lives with you ✓
- Alex is under 20 and in full-time non-advanced education (A-levels) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- No one else is claiming for Alex ✓

**For Blake (age 18):**
- You are responsible for Blake and Blake lives with you ✓
- However, Blake is 18 and is NOT in full-time non-advanced education or approved training
- Under UK rules, Child Benefit stops when a child turns 16, unless they remain in approved education or training, and can only continue until they turn 20 (or the 31st August after their 19th birthday)
- Since Blake is 18 and not in qualifying education/training, she does not meet the eligibility criteria

**My assessment:**

You **are eligible** to claim Child Benefit for **Alex**, as he is under 20 and still in full-time non-advanced education.

You **are not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for **Blake**, as she is over 16 and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit would have stopped for Blake when she either turned 16 (if she left education then) or when she finished her education/training after age 16.

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about Child Benefit eligibility?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you, that's very clear. So I can claim for Alex but not for Blake. I appreciate you explaining that to me!
